title: April mid-market SQLs hit 146, up 51% from quarterly baseline
anchor: Month-to-date April SQLs at 146 versus the Jan-Mar monthly average of 97.
connect: Q1 monthly SQL volumes ran steady: January 93, February 98, March 100.
body: Month-to-date April SQLs at 146 versus the Jan-Mar monthly average of 97. Q1 monthly SQL volumes ran steady: January 93, February 98, March 100.
trace: April mid-market SQL volume shows significant acceleration versus Q1 steady-state baseline.

title: ABM SQLs accept at 61% versus 42% for non-ABM leads
anchor: April ABM acceptance rate sits at 61.4% on 70 ABM MQLs; non-ABM at 42.1% on 76 non-ABM MQLs.
connect: ABM volume and non-ABM volume run nearly even this month at 70 and 76 respectively.
body: April ABM acceptance rate sits at 61.4% on 70 ABM MQLs; non-ABM at 42.1% on 76 non-ABM MQLs. ABM volume and non-ABM volume run nearly even this month at 70 and 76 respectively.
trace: ABM channel shows higher SDR acceptance rate with nearly equal lead volumes versus broad-market approaches.
